old_claim.note: Advanced Metallurgy (Medieval) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because primary aluminum smelting is an industrial electrochemical production operation, not a direct continuation of medieval ferrous/nonferrous metallurgy.

source_shape.source_locator: Aluminum Association describes primary production as smelting alumina to aluminum metal by the Hall-Héroult process, discovered in 1886 and still the main industrial process.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The scoped smelting operation is defined by industrial Hall-Héroult electrolysis rather than medieval metallurgy.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports removing the broad medieval metallurgy edge because the source ties the production operation to a specific industrial electrochemical process.

ontology_before: The graph made aluminum smelting depend directly on broad medieval metallurgy in addition to chemistry and electricity.
ontology_after: The graph uses the Hall-Héroult process as the direct prerequisite and leaves earlier metallurgy outside the immediate smelting operation.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: direct aluminum_smelting -> advanced_metallurgy_medieval edge removed.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped to historical metal smelting generally.
A source demonstrates medieval aluminum smelting practice as a direct prerequisite for industrial primary aluminum smelting.
short_reason: Medieval metallurgy was too broad and indirect for primary aluminum smelting.
